problems.title: Single Inheritance
problems.description: Create an Animal class with a speak() method and a Dog class that inherits from Animal and adds a bark() method. Inside main(), instantiate Dog and call speak() first, then bark(). Implement the logic in main(...) and match the expected output for the provided cases.
problems.prompt: Create an Animal class with a speak() method and a Dog class that inherits from Animal and adds a bark() method. Inside main(), instantiate Dog and call speak() first, then bark().

examples.input:
Define Animal.speak() and Dog.bark(), then run:
buddy = Dog()
buddy.speak()
buddy.bark()

problems.title: Multilevel Inheritance
problems.description: Create an Animal class with speak(), a Dog class that inherits from Animal with bark(), and a Puppy class that inherits from Dog with weep(). Inside main(), instantiate Puppy and call speak(), bark(), and weep() in that order. Implement the logic in main(...) and match the expected output for the provided cases.
problems.prompt: Create an Animal class with speak(), a Dog class that inherits from Animal with bark(), and a Puppy class that inherits from Dog with weep(). Inside main(), instantiate Puppy and call speak(), bark(), and weep() in that order.

examples.input:
Define Animal.speak(), Dog.bark(), and Puppy.weep(), then run:
tiny = Puppy()
tiny.speak()
tiny.bark()
tiny.weep()
examples.output:
Animal speaks
Dog barks
Puppy weeps

test_cases.expected_output:
Animal speaks
Dog barks
Puppy weeps
test_cases.label: Test 1

problems.title: Multiple Inheritance
problems.description: Create a Father class with height(), a Mother class with intelligence(), and a Child class that inherits from both. Inside main(), instantiate Child and call height() first, then intelligence(). Implement the logic in main(...) and match the expected output for the provided cases.
problems.prompt: Create a Father class with height(), a Mother class with intelligence(), and a Child class that inherits from both. Inside main(), instantiate Child and call height() first, then intelligence().

examples.input:
Define Father.height() and Mother.intelligence(), inherit both in Child, then run:
child = Child()
child.height()
child.intelligence()
examples.output:
Height from father
Intelligence from mother

test_cases.expected_output:
Height from father
Intelligence from mother
test_cases.label: Test 1
